topic: Joint two-sided Rosenbaum sensitivity calibration from a shared least-favorable assignment law (bridge: matched-study Gamma-sensitivity x minimax multiple testing), using the banked stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax_stat result only as substrate: construct a computable level-indexed primal-dual calibration for heterogeneous matched sets that is operational without a known-Q oracle; prove finite-sample validity against every Gamma-bounded assignment law; characterize the exact (alpha,Gamma,design) region where the joint two-sided sensitivity bound strictly improves the conventional Bonferroni-doubled pair of one-sided bounds, including exact equality at Gamma=1 and the verified strict gap 0.158 at Gamma=2; prove when the least-favorable support switches and give an algorithm/certificate usable by sensitivitymv and senfm and by two-sided sensitivity reporting. The field-level headline must be the implementable joint procedure plus its strict-improvement theorem and published-workflow comparison; the banked finite all-tests LP, pair NP theorem, support-switch examples, and oracle design-sensitivity ceiling are lemmas and must not be resubmitted as the novelty claim.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one compute an exact level-indexed joint two-sided Rosenbaum sensitivity test for heterogeneous matched sets from observed outcome statistics, optimizing both tails against one shared Gamma-bounded assignment law without a known-Q oracle?

source_refs.claim: Separate sensitivity analyses are pessimistic because they permit hidden bias to alter assignment differently across tests; sharing assignment probabilities yields a stronger joint analysis.
source_refs.author: Colin B. Fogarty and Dylan S. Small
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: The shared-bias adversarial program is efficiently convex, but targets multivariate one-sided inference and uses an asymptotic chi-bar-squared calibration.
source_refs.author: Peter L. Cohen, Matt A. Olson, and Colin B. Fogarty

source_refs.claim: For arbitrary outcomes and heterogeneous strata, the general operational calculation uses a conservative Taylor bound built from the separable approximation rather than an exact joint-tail program.
source_refs.author: Paul R. Rosenbaum

source_refs.excerpt: derive Q from those primitives rather than postulating an alternative assignment law.
the two-sided joint-calibration and practical-statistic seeds remain available for a new run.

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published matched-set methods maximize directional tails separately or coordinate prespecified statistics through large-sample standardized deviates; the arbitrary-stratum workflow remains conservative rather than exactly joint. The banked frontier is finite-sample but conditions on a fixed record and postulated Q, and its reviewer explicitly required Q to be derived from observable statistics and potential-outcome primitives. Plugging an estimated Q into the oracle rule need not preserve exact Gamma-uniform size, so the missing object is an observable, tractable primal-dual union-of-tails calibration with a certificate that covers the full assignment-law polytope.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional primal-dual program and terminating executable algorithm, built from observed matched-set scores, whose dual certificate proves finite-sample size at most alpha uniformly over every Gamma-bounded assignment law without a known-Q oracle.

writing_exemplar.why: Its introduction turns the scientific fact that one confounder governs all comparisons into a shared-adversary optimization gap and then an operational sensitivity procedure.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: matched sensitivity analysis bounds hidden assignment bias -> separate worst cases allow incompatible confounders across tests -> one shared assignment law should calibrate both tails jointly -> make that calibration exact and computable without oracle Q

method_exemplars.technique: Convert a common-confounder sensitivity problem into a quadratically constrained optimization problem and use its optimization bound for simultaneous valid inference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The two-tail problem has the same shared-assignment-law structure, but requires finite-sample level-indexed calibration instead of a Gaussian QCQP bound.

method_exemplars.technique: Exploit separability across heterogeneous strata, enumerate structured extreme assignment candidates, and certify a conservative global bound by concavity and a Taylor inequality.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest operational arbitrary-stratum workflow and exposes precisely where an exact primal-dual joint-tail replacement must improve the approximation.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct a nonasymptotic distribution-free adaptive signed-rank test and prove sensitivity validity uniformly over a statistic family.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its finite-sample uniform-calibration proof is a template for replacing an oracle least-favorable law by a computable worst-case certificate, although its data regime is paired.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the exact alpha-Gamma-design region in heterogeneous matched sets where a shared-law joint two-sided Rosenbaum sensitivity bound strictly improves the Bonferroni-doubled pair of separately worst-cased one-sided bounds?

source_refs.claim: The paper shows that enforcing common hidden-assignment probabilities can strictly improve on combining separately optimized sensitivity analyses, but does not characterize the complete strictness region for opposite tails.
source_refs.author: Colin B. Fogarty and Dylan S. Small
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: The paper supplies sensitivity tests and intervals for M-statistics in pairs and variable-control matched sets, forming the methodological basis of sensitivitymv and senfm-style one-sided workflows.
source_refs.author: Paul R. Rosenbaum

source_refs.claim: The adversarial-game formulation quantifies gains from coherent shared-bias combinations, while its theory remains directional and asymptotic rather than an exact two-tail equality-versus-strictness theorem.
source_refs.author: Peter L. Cohen, Matt A. Olson, and Colin B. Fogarty

source_refs.excerpt: the exact joint two-sided improvement region
verified strict gap 0.158 at Gamma=2, exact equality at Gamma=1

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published shared-confounder work proves the qualitative max-min reason that separate worst cases can be conservative and exhibits possible strict improvement, while operational matched-set reporting still forms a two-sided bound from two one-sided analyses. Neither literature gives the exact geometry of equality versus strict gain as alpha, Gamma, set sizes, and score configurations vary. The bank records one Gamma=2 witness and the Gamma=1 equality point, but it did not define and solve the full joint two-sided game or derive necessary-and-sufficient inequalities valid over every Gamma-bounded assignment law.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An exact finite-sample joint two-sided calibration formula with necessary-and-sufficient inequalities partitioning the alpha-by-Gamma-by-design space into equality and strict-dominance regions, including a proof of equality at Gamma=1 and an analytic certificate for the 0.158 gap at Gamma=2.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames conservatism as the reversal between combining separately optimized tests and optimizing once under the shared assignment adversary.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: one hidden assignment mechanism governs both rejection directions -> separate directional maxima enlarge the adversary -> shared-law calibration can improve power -> characterize exactly when the two-sided gain is strict

method_exemplars.technique: Compare shared-law and separately optimized sensitivity criteria through their primal optimization values and derive simultaneous inference from the common-law program.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired result refines its possible-strict-inequality phenomenon into exact necessary and sufficient conditions for the two directional tails.

method_exemplars.technique: Represent analyst-versus-adversary testing as convex optimization over coherent combinations and derive the optimized statistic's null calibration.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The positive and negative tails form the smallest opposing coherent family, so the cone and KKT geometry can guide the exact finite-sample partition.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct least-favorable distributions and likelihood-ratio rules through convex robust-testing duality, then certify the saddle point by primal-dual inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Equality versus strict gain is governed by whether both directional risks share a saddle-point least-favorable law or require incompatible separate adversaries.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the least-favorable support and every support switch for joint two-sided Gamma calibration be computed over heterogeneous matched sets by a terminating primal-dual algorithm with a compact validity certificate, without enumerating the full product-vertex family?

source_refs.claim: Minimax detector construction reduces to determining least-favorable distributions, and robust testing with multiple hypotheses differs fundamentally from the binary problem.
source_refs.author: Michael Fauß, Abdelhak M. Zoubir, and H. Vincent Poor

source_refs.claim: Worst-case heterogeneous-stratum calculations reduce to structured extreme assignment configurations, but the published general method certifies a conservative approximation rather than an exact support map.
source_refs.author: Paul R. Rosenbaum

source_refs.claim: A shared-bias sensitivity analysis can be posed as an efficiently solvable convex program, but it does not provide a finite-sample support-switch theorem or portable certificate for opposing tails.
source_refs.author: Peter L. Cohen, Matt A. Olson, and Colin B. Fogarty

source_refs.excerpt: The field-tier promise requires an implementable level-indexed least-favorable procedure
as written it is a useful finite diagnostic, not a demonstrated field-opening Stat advance.

open_problems.why_unsolved: The bank proves a finite full-vertex LP characterization and small support-switch witnesses, but its null family is a product of local Gamma vertices whose explicit enumeration grows exponentially; its self-selected terminal-block equality is a diagnostic, not a scalable algorithmic guarantee. Robust-testing theory supplies saddle-point conditions, and matched-study work supplies structured extreme configurations and convex programs, but neither specializes these tools into a parametric alpha-Gamma support-cell decomposition with a separation oracle and independently checkable certificate usable by heterogeneous-set sensitivity software.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A column-generation, separation-oracle, or equivalent primal-dual algorithm with finite termination and correctness proofs that outputs the rejection rule, least-favorable support, all alpha-Gamma switch cells, and a complementary-slackness or primal-dual-gap certificate of validity against every Gamma-bounded product assignment law.

writing_exemplar.why: Its analyst-versus-nature framing makes changes in the active adversarial assignment law natural and connects the theory to a computable matched-study procedure.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: sensitivity analysis is a game against hidden assignment bias -> opposing tails create competing adversarial objectives -> the active least-favorable law can switch with level and Gamma -> compute every regime and certify it without exhaustive enumeration

method_exemplars.technique: Use saddle-point and least-favorable-distribution conditions to derive robust tests and identify the active density constraints.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A support-switch theorem is a specialization of least-favorable-distribution KKT geometry to the finite Gamma-bounded assignment polytope.

method_exemplars.technique: Exploit matched-stratum separability and a finite structured family of extreme propensity configurations to compute worst-case p-value bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Those structured extremes are the candidate columns whose active regions and transitions the new algorithm must discover and certify.

method_exemplars.technique: Use common-confounder optimization and duality to coordinate worst-case assignments across several test statistics.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired software certificate can adapt its shared-law optimization while exposing which assignment constraints and supports are active.

literature_map:
Rosenbaum (1987, Biometrika) introduced Gamma sensitivity bounds for permutation inference in matched observational studies.
Rosenbaum (1988, Biometrika) extended the framework to multiple controls for a restricted generalized signed-rank family.
Gastwirth, Krieger, and Rosenbaum (2000, JRSSB) developed the separable approximation underlying many operational one-sided matched-set calculations.
Rosenbaum (2007, Biometrics) developed M-tests, intervals, and estimates for pairs and variable-control matched sets; these methods underpin sensitivitymv and senfm workflows.
The senfm documentation explicitly performs one-sided bounds and recommends a two-sided Bonferroni construction; it reports little conservatism at Gamma=1 and increasing conservatism for larger Gamma.
Fogarty and Small (2016, JASA; arXiv:1505.03810) showed that separate sensitivity analyses can be pessimistic because they allow incompatible hidden-bias assignments across tests.
Their common-confounder QCQP proves shared-law improvement and possible strict inequality, but it does not characterize the full opposite-tail strictness region.
Rosenbaum (2018, AOAS; DOI 10.1214/18-AOAS1153) made arbitrary-stratum calculations feasible through a conservative Taylor bound from a separable near-extreme solution.
Cohen, Olson, and Fogarty (2020, Biometrika; arXiv:1812.07728) formulated multivariate one-sided sensitivity testing as an adversarial convex game with an asymptotic chi-bar-squared law.
That framework coordinates shared hidden bias, but its headline theory is directional and asymptotic rather than an exact finite-sample two-tail calibration.
Howard and Pimentel (2021, Biometrika; arXiv:1904.08895) provide a nonasymptotic distribution-free adaptive sensitivity test, but only for paired signed-rank data.
Fogarty (2020, JASA; arXiv:1609.02112) supplies studentized weak-null sensitivity inference for paired studies with heterogeneous effects.
Fogarty (2023, Biometrics; arXiv:1908.07352) proves a validity-versus-sharpness obstruction for weak-null sensitivity analysis in general matched designs.
Fauß, Zoubir, and Poor (2021, IEEE TSP; arXiv:2105.09836) show that minimax tests hinge on least-favorable distributions and that robust multiple-hypothesis testing differs from the binary case.
The live gap is an exact operational shared-law two-tail calibration with a strict-improvement region, support-switch theorem, and software-checkable primal-dual certificate.
prior_proposal_map:
The highest-priority source was the downgraded bank entry stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax_stat, whose README explicitly reserves the two-sided joint-calibration and practical-statistic seeds for a new run.
Its field-tier promise was not met: the achieved result is a finite full-vertex diagnostic and fixed-Q oracle ceiling, while an implementable level-indexed procedure and exact joint improvement region remain open.
All four angle reviews were inspected; angle0_v1 strengthening_paths[0] directly prescribes rebuilding the target around an observed statistic and potential-outcome alternative that derives Q.
angle0_v2 and angle0_v3 repeat the need for an explicit generative alternative and practical Rosenbaum-specific formulation; their m=3 support-switch requests were subsequently solved and are substrate, not new gaps.
The README records the verified Gamma=2 gap of 0.158 and equality at Gamma=1 but says the general two-sided theorem was not established.
No rejected-angle TeX file exists in this bank entry, and the active target qid currently contains orchestration state rather than a prior proposal.
A catalogue-wide text check found no second matched-study Rosenbaum proposal with an overlapping joint two-sided or least-favorable-assignment kernel.
